errors: [ValueSet "Reason for not performing Chlamydia Screening" is marked as TBD and not defined, which is required for clinical accuracy. The logic for "No Screening" includes a 1-year lookback period, but the CPG may require specific documentation of reasons for not screening, which is incomplete without the defined value set.]

[Time Interval for Colonoscopy Lookback is 1 month, but clinical guidelines typically recommend a 10-year interval for average-risk colon cancer screening. This mismatch may lead to incorrect recommendations for patients who recently had a colonoscopy but are due for another screening.]

[Age threshold of 18 years is reasonable for adult diabetes diagnosis, but HbA1c threshold of 9 g% is clinically incorrect. The CPG typically defines diabetes diagnosis at HbA1c ≥6.5%, not 9%. Additionally, the "Time Intervals" section incorrectly lists 9 g% as a time interval, which is a concentration value, not a duration.]

[The ELM implementation does not include the 20 pack-year smoking history criterion required by clinical guidelines. The "Has Smoking History" condition only checks for active tobacco use status but does not verify the pack-year duration, which is a critical component of lung cancer screening eligibility criteria.]

[Age threshold of 30 years does not align with standard clinical guidelines (e.g., CDC recommends HIV screening for ages 13-64). The 1-year lookback period for recent HIV testing is clinically reasonable, but the age threshold may be inconsistent with general population recommendations.]

[Age thresholds in "In Demographic" start at 30 years instead of 21 years as recommended by USPSTF for cervical cancer screening. The CPG specifies screening for women aged 21-65, but the ELM implementation only includes ages 30-65.]

[Age threshold is set to 12 years in the ELM, but USPSTF depression screening recommendations typically apply to adults aged 18 and older. The age threshold should be >=18 years for adult populations unless the CPG explicitly includes adolescents (12-17 years) as a target group, which is not clearly stated in the provided CPG summary.]

[Age threshold in ELM is set to 25 years, but the CPG (USPSTF) recommends screening for adults 18 years or older. This discrepancy in age criteria may exclude eligible patients aged 18-24 who should be screened according to clinical guidelines.]

[1. The "Has Osteoporotic Fracture" condition does not verify the clinical status is active, potentially including resolved fractures. 2. The DEXA scan value set OID (2.16.840.1.113762.1.4.1151.38) may not align with standard LOINC codes for DEXA (e.g., 5702-2). 3. The Female Administrative Sex OID (2.16.840.1.113883.3.560.100.2) requires confirmation to ensure it maps to "female" as per standard terminology systems.]

[Age threshold in ELM is 55-75 years, but the CPG specifies 65-75 years for AAA screening in men. The In Demographic logic uses age >=55 instead of >=65, which is inconsistent with the USPSTF recommendation stated in the Rationale.]

[MeetsInclusionCriteria logic incorrectly uses "OR" between Screening Age and Gender. It should be "AND" to ensure only female patients aged >=40 are included. The current logic would include all female patients regardless of age if they lack a recent mammogram, which is not clinically appropriate.]

[The ELM implementation is missing the 20 pack-year smoking history requirement in the "Has Smoking History" logic. The CPG specifies that patients must have a 20 pack-year smoking history, but the ELM only checks for active tobacco use (ValueSet) without verifying pack-years.]

[The lookback period for Colonoscopy is set to 1 month, which is clinically unreasonable. Colonoscopy screening is typically recommended every 10 years for average-risk individuals. A 1-month lookback would incorrectly identify patients who recently had a colonoscopy as needing another procedure, rather than ensuring they are due for screening based on appropriate intervals.]

[Needs Falls Prevention Screening logic is inverted. The ELM defines it as In Demographic AND Has Recent Falls Assessment, but the correct clinical logic should be In Demographic AND NOT Has Recent Falls Assessment. This would incorrectly trigger recommendations for patients who already have a recent falls assessment.]
